sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Conagra Brands names J.M. Smucker executive John Brase as new CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Brase, formerly president and COO of J.M. Smucker Co, as its new CEO. He will succeed Sean Connolly, who is stepping down after over a decade at the helm. Brase is set to join the board of directors and assume the CEO role on June 1, 2026.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Q1 earnings beat, but NII, credit loss provision disappoint (GS:NYSE)
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs' Q1 earnings per share and total revenue surpassed analyst expectations; however, net interest income fell short, and credit loss provisions were higher than anticipated, causing a premarket stock dip of 3.5%. The institutional banking and markets division was a strong performer.

feed.title: CHIPOTLE RELAUNCHES REWARDS WITH "REWARDS ON REPEAT," DELIVERING MORE VALUE WITHOUT TRADE-OFFS
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has relaunched its Rewards program as "Rewards on Repeat," aiming to provide more frequent rewards, greater flexibility, and deeper engagement for its 21 million active members. New benefits include monthly free food drops, expanded redemption options, and extended points expiration, with a focus on immediate value and personalization. The program also features a redesigned in-app experience and an in-restaurant campaign to boost enrollment.

feed.title: Medtronic Signs Distribution Deal With Merit Medical for FDA-Cleared ViaVerte Back Pain System
feed.summary: Medtronic has partnered with Merit Medical Systems to distribute the FDA-cleared ViaVerte system, an implant-free basivertebral nerve ablation (BVNA) system for chronic vertebrogenic lower back pain. This agreement expands Medtronic's pain management portfolio with a minimally invasive, steerable BVNA platform. The collaboration aims to address the rising demand for less invasive pain interventions for the large segment of the U.S. population affected by chronic back pain.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Announces New CEO and Leadership Transition
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has announced that John Brase will become its new President and CEO, effective June 1, 2026, succeeding Sean Connolly. Brase, a veteran consumer goods executive, will join the board and receive a performance-based compensation package. This leadership transition aims to ensure continuity in Conagra's growth strategy in branded foods and strengthen its competitive position.

feed.title: Chipotle Relaunches Rewards Program as ‘Rewards on Repeat’
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has relaunched its loyalty program as "Rewards on Repeat" in the U.S. and Canada, offering enhanced benefits and a redesigned in-app experience. The program aims to provide more frequent rewards, greater flexibility, and deeper engagement, particularly targeting younger demographics who are increasingly influenced by loyalty perks. Key updates include immediate rewards for new members, monthly free food drops, customizable birthday rewards, expanded redemption options, and an always-on gamification system.

feed.title: Fastenal, Replimune Group, Children's Place And Other Big Stocks Moving Lower In Monday's Pre-Market Session
feed.summary: U.S. stock futures were lower on Monday, with several companies experiencing significant drops in pre-market trading. Fastenal Co (NASDAQ: FAST) fell after reporting mixed first-quarter results, while Replimune Group Inc (NASDAQ: REPL) plunged due to an FDA decision regarding its Biologics License Application. Children's Place Inc (NASDAQ: PLCE) also saw a substantial decline after posting weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results.

feed.title: U.S. Capital Wealth Advisors LLC Increases Stake in Citigroup Inc. $C
feed.summary: U.S. Capital Wealth Advisors LLC significantly increased its stake in Citigroup Inc. during the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 10,381 shares, bringing its total holdings to 49,020 shares valued at approximately $5.72 million. This comes despite an insider, Cantu Ernesto Torres, selling a large portion of his shares. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for Citigroup, with a target price of $127.41, while the company trades near $124 and recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.60 per share.

feed.title: AbbVie (ABBV) Ends Development Agreement, CollPlant Eyes New Opp
feed.summary: AbbVie has ended its five-year development agreement with CollPlant for a dermal and soft tissue filler, leading CollPlant to seek new partnerships in the aesthetics market. AbbVie's strategic shift opens new opportunities for both companies, although AbbVie's high P/E ratio and recent insider selling activity suggest caution for investors despite its strong GF Score™ in profitability and growth.

feed.title: Microchip Strengthens Global ASA‑ML Ecosystem Through Strategic Collaboration with Leading Camera Manufacturer Sunny Smartlead
feed.summary: Microchip Technology and Sunny Smartlead have announced a strategic collaboration to expand the Automotive SerDes Alliance Motion Link (ASA-ML) ecosystem. Sunny Smartlead will introduce ADAS camera modules built on Microchip’s VS700 family of ASA-ML devices, aiming to simplify and reduce the cost of camera development for the automotive industry. This partnership highlights the growing maturity of the ASA-ML ecosystem, offering secure, high-speed, and standardized ADAS camera solutions for Software-Defined Vehicles.

feed.title: Fifth Third’s Newline Extends MCP Server Capabilities with Skills
feed.summary: Fifth Third's embedded finance business, Newline, has introduced "Skills" to its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server, enhancing AI model capabilities. This new layer standardizes how AI models use tools and workflows, promoting faster automation, intelligent system performance, and stronger governance. The development aims to help enterprises safely deploy AI at scale by securely connecting AI models to external resources and data.

feed.title: Compeer Financial Transforms Agricultural Lending with FICO Platform
feed.summary: Compeer Financial has transformed its agricultural lending operations using FICO Platform, leading to faster and more accurate credit decisions. The member-owned Farm Credit cooperative increased automated lending application throughput to over $10 billion and significantly reduced underwriter time, earning it a 2026 FICO® Industry Vanguard Award. This technological advancement allows Compeer to better serve farmers and agribusinesses with rapid access to capital while maintaining rigorous risk standards.

feed.title: Stryker signs definitive agreement to acquire Amplitude
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems, Inc. (AVS), a medical technology company specializing in intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) for calcified peripheral arterial disease. This acquisition will enhance Stryker’s Peripheral Vascular portfolio by adding next-generation revascularization technology, expanding its offerings in arterial disease treatment. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions.

feed.title: Stryker targets next-gen artery treatment with Amplitude deal
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems, aiming to enhance its peripheral vascular portfolio with next-generation intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) technology. This acquisition focuses on treating calcified peripheral arterial disease using pulsed CO2-generated pressure waves. The deal is subject to customary closing conditions, and both companies will operate separately until the transaction is complete, with the new technology requiring market clearance before commercial deployment.

feed.title: Enphase Energy Expands Commercial Microinverter Deployments Across the United States
feed.summary: Enphase Energy is expanding its commercial microinverter deployments across the United States, utilizing products like the IQ9N-3P and IQ8P-3P Commercial Microinverters manufactured in U.S. facilities. These products are designed for three-phase commercial solar systems, offering high efficiency, grid compliance, and domestic content advantages for tax credits and federal projects. Installers are reporting benefits such as enhanced safety, reduced installation time, and reliable performance compared to traditional string inverters, even for repowering existing systems.

feed.title: Teledyne Recognizes 15 Years of Providing the U.S. Navy SEAL Delivery Vehicle Fleet
feed.summary: Teledyne Brown Engineering, a subsidiary of Teledyne Technologies, is celebrating 15 years as the prime contractor for the U.S. Special Operations Command's MK 11 SEAL Delivery Vehicle (SDV). The company has secured over $425 million in contracts for the MK 11 SDV and is now expanding its support to allied and partner nations through Foreign Military Sales. The MK 11 SDV, also known as the Shallow Water Combat Submersible (SWCS), clandestinely delivers Special Operations Forces into high-threat environments, with work performed at Teledyne Brown Engineering’s Huntsville, Alabama facility.

feed.title: The J.M. Smucker Company stock (US8326961037): Why its pet food and coffee positioning matters more
feed.summary: The J.M. Smucker Company (SJM) is positioned for resilient growth due to its strong portfolio of household names in coffee, pet food, and snacks. The company's strategic expansions into pet nutrition and away-from-home channels, coupled with its emphasis on value-oriented brands and consistent dividends, make it an attractive investment, especially to income-focused investors. While coffee remains a major revenue driver, pet foods are a key growth engine, aligning with the "pet humanization" trend and offering diversification benefits that balance the company's performance.

feed.title: Conagra Brands names J.M. Smucker executive John Brase as new CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Brase, formerly of J.M. Smucker, as its new CEO, effective June 1, 2026. Brase, 58, will succeed Sean Connolly, who is stepping down after more than a decade with the packaged food maker. Connolly will leave his leadership roles and the company's board on May 31.

feed.title: Halliburton wins multibillion-dollar contract from YPF for Vaca Muerta work (HAL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Halliburton (HAL) has been awarded a multibillion-dollar contract by YPF (YPF) to provide unconventional completions services in Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale play. This exclusive contract led to a 1.8% pre-market increase in Halliburton's stock price on Monday.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Purchases 9,115 Shares of Welltower Inc. $WELL
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue increased its stake in Welltower Inc. (NYSE:WELL) by 2.3% in Q4, acquiring 9,115 additional shares to hold a total of 408,406 shares, making it their 13th-largest position. Welltower reported Q4 EPS of $1.45, beating estimates, with revenue of $3.18 billion, a 41.3% year-over-year increase. The company trades near $207 with a market cap of $144.8 billion and offers an annual dividend of $2.96, yielding approximately 1.4%.

feed.title: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. stock (US38141G1040): Is investment banking strength now the real test
feed.summary: This article analyzes The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (US38141G1040) stock, highlighting its position as a leading investment bank benefiting from volatile markets and M&A activity. It discusses Goldman Sachs' core business model focused on high-margin activities, its dominance in U.S. capital markets, and its competitive edge against peers. The piece also details strategic initiatives, positive analyst sentiment, and the inherent risks associated with market volatility and regulation for this U.S.-centric financial powerhouse.

feed.title: Otis buys majority stake in WeMaintain to bolster tech-driven service (OTIS:NYSE)
feed.summary: Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) announced its acquisition of a majority stake in WeMaintain, a technology-enabled elevator and escalator service company. This strategic move highlights Otis's commitment to enhancing its tech-driven service capabilities. The financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb evolves and expands multiple myeloma programme
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) has expanded its "Standing in the Gaap" program with a large-scale survey in the US to identify gaps in multiple myeloma care. This initiative, developed with several foundations, aims to gather perspectives from over 1,000 patients, caregivers, and healthcare providers to inform future programming and address health inequities. The program, created in 2016, seeks to ensure care pathways are culturally responsive and community-informed.

feed.title: AT&T Inc. $T Shares Sold by State of Alaska Department of Revenue
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T) by 5.8% in the fourth quarter, selling 60,825 shares and now holding 984,578 shares valued at $24.46 million. AT&T recently exceeded Q4 earnings expectations with $0.52 EPS and $33.47 billion in revenue, provided a positive FY2026 guidance, and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2775. The stock currently carries a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $30.74.

feed.title: Victory Financial Group LLC Has $9.40 Million Stake in Microsoft Corporation $MSFT
feed.summary: Victory Financial Group LLC significantly increased its stake in Microsoft Corporation, bringing its total holdings to $9.401 million. This increase comes alongside strong earnings reports from Microsoft, driven by Azure growth and AI momentum, leading analysts to maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $586.26. However, some insider selling and competitive pressures in the AI space are noted as potential short-term concerns.

feed.title: Willis Investment Counsel Has $75.62 Million Position in Johnson & Johnson $JNJ
feed.summary: Willis Investment Counsel has increased its stake in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) by 1.5%, bringing its total holding to 365,399 shares valued at $75.62 million, making it their largest holding. Several other institutional investors also increased their positions in J&J, indicating broad institutional interest despite mixed but mostly positive Wall Street sentiment. The article highlights positive catalysts such as the VARIPULSE device launch and J&J's long dividend streak, alongside risks like litigation and biosimilar competition.

feed.title: Tectonic Advisors LLC Acquires 40,332 Shares of VICI Properties Inc. $VICI
feed.summary: Tectonic Advisors LLC has increased its stake in VICI Properties Inc. by 12.5%, purchasing 40,332 additional shares to bring its total holdings to 364,050 shares, valued at approximately $10.24 million. This comes as VICI Properties reported Q4 earnings that missed analyst consensus but showed a 3.8% year-over-year revenue increase, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.45 per share, representing a 6.4% yield. The company's stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price of $33.69.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Cuts Stock Holdings in Bank of America Corporation $BAC
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) by 8.8% in Q4, selling 64,730 shares and retaining 667,258 shares valued at approximately $36.7 million. This move coincides with notable insider selling activities, with insiders divesting 227,832 shares worth about $11.09 million in the last quarter, reducing insider ownership to 0.27%. Despite the reductions by some investors, Bank of America reported strong Q4 results, beating EPS estimates and seeing a 12.3% year-over-year revenue increase, maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts with a target price of $59.10.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 4,185 Shares of Lowe's Companies, Inc. $LOW
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue sold 4,185 shares of Lowe's Companies, Inc., reducing its stake by 3.5% to 114,950 shares valued at $27.72 million. This comes after Lowe's reported stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings and provided a positive FY2026 guidance, while also declaring a quarterly dividend of $1.20. Despite the reduction from the State of Alaska, institutional ownership remains high at 74.06%, with several other major funds significantly increasing their positions in the home improvement retailer.

feed.title: Bionano Genomics stock price target lowered to $6 by H.C. Wainwright
feed.summary: H.C. Wainwright has lowered its price target for Bionano Genomics (NASDAQ:BNGO) to $6 from $7, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment is primarily due to an increased share count, despite the stock trading near its 52-week low and an InvestingPro analysis suggesting it may be undervalued. Bionano Genomics reported a revenue decline for Q4 2025 and the full year 2025, but management provided 2026 revenue guidance indicating potential topline growth of up to 16%.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Has $24.70 Million Stake in The TJX Companies, Inc. $TJX
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue has reported a $24.70 million stake in The TJX Companies, Inc. despite trimming its holdings by 5.1% in the fourth quarter of the prior fiscal year. TJX Companies beat Q4 earnings and revenue expectations, raising its quarterly dividend and setting positive FY2027 guidance. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating for the stock.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 15,192 Shares of PepsiCo, Inc. $PEP
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in PepsiCo, Inc. by 8.1% in the fourth quarter, selling 15,192 shares and holding 171,384 shares valued at approximately $24.6 million. Despite this, PepsiCo's board approved a $10 billion share buyback and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.4225, yielding 3.6%. The company slightly surpassed Q4 earnings and revenue consensus estimates, though analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with a target price of $168.47.

feed.title: Cisco Systems, Inc. $CSCO Position Decreased by State of Alaska Department of Revenue
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its holdings in Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) by 5.7% in the fourth quarter, selling 31,330 shares. This move leaves the department with 521,384 shares valued at approximately $40.16 million, making Cisco its 29th-largest holding. Despite this decrease by one institutional investor, other hedge funds increased their positions, and Cisco reported strong Q4 earnings, beating revenue and EPS estimates, while also raising its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Capital One Financial, Amazon And Nvidia: CNBC’s Final Trades
feed.summary: Bryn Talkington recommends Capital One Financial, citing upcoming earnings as a potential catalyst. Kevin Simpson picked Amazon, with Cantor Fitzgerald maintaining an Overweight rating and raising the price target to $260. Jim Lebenthal stated that NVIDIA's post-earnings selloff is over and expects the stock to reach $200, while Raymond James maintains a Strong Buy rating and raised its price target to $323.

feed.title: Tectonic Advisors LLC Boosts Stock Holdings in Paychex, Inc. $PAYX
feed.summary: Tectonic Advisors LLC increased its stake in Paychex, Inc. (NASDAQ:PAYX) by 17.4% in the fourth quarter of the previous year, now holding 79,708 shares valued at approximately $8.94 million. Despite Paychex beating quarterly earnings and revenue expectations and authorizing a $1.0 billion stock buyback, analysts maintain a cautious "Reduce" rating with price target cuts. Institutional ownership of Paychex stands at around 83.5%.

feed.title: Medtronic to announce financial results for its fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2026
feed.summary: Medtronic plc (NYSE: MDT) will report its financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2026 on Wednesday, June 3, 2026. The fiscal year concludes on April 24, 2026. A news release will be issued at 5:45 a.m. CT, followed by a video webcast at 6:45 a.m. CT to discuss the results.

feed.title: Tectonic Advisors LLC Has $2.53 Million Holdings in Diamondback Energy, Inc. $FANG
feed.summary: Tectonic Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ: FANG) by 45.5% in Q4, ending the period with holdings valued at $2.533 million. Despite substantial insider selling totalling over $200 million in the past 90 days, analysts maintain a largely positive outlook with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average target price of $203.95. Diamondback Energy also recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.05 per share.

feed.title: AdvanSix names Patrick Day as CFO effective April 27 By Investing.com
feed.summary: AdvanSix (NYSE:ASIX) has appointed Patrick C. Day as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective April 27, 2026. Day, previously with FMC Corporation, will report to CEO Erin Kane. This announcement follows a nearly 40% year-to-date increase in AdvanSix's stock, though the company recently missed Q4 2025 earnings expectations.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Extends Lease Agreements for Santa Clara Properties
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW) has extended lease agreements for four properties in Santa Clara, California, encompassing over 940,000 square feet. The extensions are for twelve years, starting August 1, 2028, with an initial rent abatement period followed by a base rent of $3.825 per square foot with annual 2% increases. This strategic move, which includes a tenant improvement allowance, aims to enhance the company's operational capacity and infrastructure.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 32,215 Shares of Verizon Communications Inc. $VZ
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Verizon Communications Inc. by selling 32,215 shares in the fourth quarter, now holding 629,933 shares valued at $25.66 million. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in VZ. The company recently reported earnings of $1.09 per share, beating estimates, and announced an increased quarterly dividend of $0.7075.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Cuts Position in Gilead Sciences, Inc. $GILD
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue decreased its stake in Gilead Sciences by 5.4% in the fourth quarter, selling 9,210 shares. Despite this, analyst sentiment remains largely bullish, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average target price of $156.42. Gilead is actively expanding its oncology pipeline through acquisitions and licensing, reported a slight earnings beat, and raised its FY2026 EPS guidance while increasing its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Lowers Holdings in S&P Global Inc. $SPGI
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) by 8.7% in Q4, selling 2,920 shares and retaining 30,804 shares valued at $16.10 million. Despite this, S&P Global reported a 9% year-over-year revenue increase to $3.92 billion in Q4, provided strong FY2026 EPS guidance, and increased its quarterly dividend. Analysts largely maintain a "Buy" rating for the stock, with an average target price of $558.80.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Decreases Stock Holdings in Danaher Corporation $DHR
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Danaher Corporation by 8.2% in Q4, selling 5,615 shares and retaining 62,500 shares valued at approximately $14.31 million. Danaher recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.40 per share and reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $251.35.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Decreases Stake in The Charles Schwab Corporation $SCHW
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue has reduced its stake in The Charles Schwab Corporation by 9.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 17,610 shares. Despite this, Charles Schwab reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates with $1.39 EPS and $6.34 billion in revenue, and increased its quarterly dividend. The company holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $114.50, though some analysts have recently trimmed their price targets.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 13,915 Shares of NextEra Energy, Inc. $NEE
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue recently reduced its stake in NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE: NEE) by 6.3%, selling 13,915 shares, which leaves them with 206,899 shares valued at $16.61 million. This sale comes as the company's insiders also sold shares, totaling over $17 million in the last quarter. Despite these sales, NextEra Energy has seen its dividend increase and exceeded EPS estimates in its latest earnings, maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 59,405 Shares of Bristol Myers Squibb Company $BMY
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) by 18.7%, selling 59,405 shares, now holding 258,405 shares valued at $13.94 million. Concurrently, several major institutions significantly increased their positions in BMY, raising institutional ownership to 76.41%. Despite a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts and a recent EPS miss, the stock offers a 4.3% dividend yield, but faces caution due to generic erosion and Q1 earnings risk.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Has $13.35 Million Stock Position in Arista Networks, Inc. $ANET
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue recently decreased its holdings in Arista Networks by 8.2% in the fourth quarter, selling 9,055 shares but still holding 101,916 shares valued at $13.35 million. Arista Networks reported a strong financial quarter, exceeding analyst expectations with $0.82 EPS and $2.49 billion in revenue. Despite institutional buying, company insiders have been net sellers, with significant share sales by Kenneth Duda and Kelly Bodnar Battles totaling over $26 million in the last 90 days.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Trims Position in McDonald's Corporation $MCD
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in McDonald's Corporation by 7.1% in the fourth quarter, selling 5,445 shares and retaining 71,030 shares valued at approximately $21.71 million. This comes amid significant insider selling by executives, with CEO Christopher Kempczinski and EVP Jonathan Banner offloading shares. Despite new product announcements like energy drinks on U.S. menus, McDonald's shares have dropped over 6% in the past month, and the consensus analyst rating is a "Hold" with an average target price near $339.69.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 3,960 Shares of Linde PLC $LIN
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Linde PLC by 7.9%, selling 3,960 shares and retaining 46,202 shares valued at approximately $19.7 million. This sale comes amidst other institutional investor activity in Linde, insider selling by company executives, and generally bullish analyst ratings with a consensus "Buy" and price target of $518.10. Linde also recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.60 per share.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Trims Stock Position in Analog Devices, Inc. $ADI
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) by 9.8% in Q4 2025, selling 5,325 shares and retaining 48,914 shares valued at $13.27 million. Institutional investors collectively own 86.81% of the stock. Several analysts have raised their price targets for Analog Devices, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $367.81.
